title: lithocarpus tawaiensis
intro: Lithocarpus tawaiensis, also known as the Tawia Nut tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Philippines. The plant's bark, leaves, and nuts have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Lithocarpus tawaiensis include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparations of the plant typically involve decoctions, infusions, or extracts of its bark, leaves, and nuts, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments and promote overall well-being.
